key_insight: A CF/PC rivet plus ultrasonic welding and TC4 surface engineering creates a stronger CFRTP-to-titanium joint while enabling real-time resistance-based process and damage monitoring.
engineering_relevance: The approach offers a fast, adhesive-free, metal-fastener-free joining route for lightweight aerospace structures with built-in quality assessment.
method: Hot-rivet a same-material CF/PC rivet and ultrasonic-weld the CFRTP–TC4 interface in one cycle.
Improve bonding with TC4 surface grooving and anodic oxidation to promote mechanical interlocking and interfacial interaction.
Measure interfacial resistance during welding and loading to track joining stages and damage evolution.
evidence_signal: Compared with direct welding, the joint shear strength increased by 11.08% and cross-lap tensile strength by 128.6%, with resistance stages aligning to interfacial evolution.
watch_item: The resistance-to-strength model is only preliminary, so calibration and validation across geometries, materials, and loading conditions will be needed.
paper_title: Interfacial joining and in-situ resistance monitoring in ultrasonic hybrid riveting–welding of CFRTP and titanium alloy
